devanagari: ऋषिगणकृता भगवत्स्तुतिः वसुदेवयज्ञोत्सवः बन्धूनां प्रस्थापनादिकं च । श्रीशुक उवाच । श्रुत्वा पृथा सुबलपुत्र्यथ याज्ञसेनी । माधव्यथ क्षित...
english_devnagari: ṛṣigaṇakṛtā bhagavatstutiḥ vasudevayajñotsavaḥ bandhūnāṃ prasthāpanādikaṃ caśrīśuka uvācaśrutvā pṛthā subalaputryatha yājñasenīmādhavyatha kṣitipapat‍nya uta svagopyaḥkṛṣṇe'khilātmani harau praṇayānubandhaṃsarvā visismyuralamaśrukalākulākṣyaḥ
translation: Droṇa, Bhīṣma, Karṇa, Bāhlīka, Vidura and others and Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with his sons returned to their respective homes severally.

devanagari: इति सम्भाषमाणासु स्त्रीभिः स्त्रीषु नृभिर्नृषु । आययुर्मुनयस्तत्र कृष्णरामदिदृक्षया ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sambhāṣamāṇāsu strībhiḥ strīṣu nṛbhirnṛṣuāyayurmunayastatra kṛṣṇarāmadidṛkṣayā
translation: All other kings who had come there on a holy pilgrimage (stayed there for three months at the end of which) they returned to their respective kingdoms.

devanagari: द्वैपायनो नारदश्च च्यवनो देवलोऽसितः । विश्वामित्रः शतानन्दो भरद्वाजोऽथ गौतमः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dvaipāyano nāradaśca cyavano devalo'sitaḥviśvāmitraḥ śatānando bharadvājo'tha gautamaḥ
translation: And the noble-souled Pāṇḍavas and Kuntī, speaking in faltering accents (as their voice was choked with tears) accompanied with all their relatives, returned to Indraprastha, taking leave of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: रामः सशिष्यो भगवान् वसिष्ठो गालवो भृगुः । पुलस्त्यः कश्यपोऽत्रिश्च मार्कण्डेयो बृहस्पतिः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: rāmaḥ saśiṣyo bhagavān vasiṣṭho gālavo bhṛguḥpulastyaḥ kaśyapo'triśca mārkaṇḍeyo bṛhaspatiḥ
translation: (They were as follows:) Kṛṣṇa-Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), the divine sage Nārada, Cyavana, Devala, Asita, Viśvāmitra, Śatānanda, Bhāradvāja. Gautama, Paraśurāma along with his disciples, the worshipful Vasiṣṭha, Gālava, Bhṛgu, Pulastya, Kaśyapa, Atri, Mārkaṇḍeya, Bṛhaspati, Dvita, Trita, Ekata, sons of god Brahmā (such as Sanaka, Sanandana and others) Aṅgiras, Agastya, Yājñavalkya and others.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Under ‘others’ VT. includes Parāśara, Maitreya, Vaiśampāyana, etc. Historically these were not contemporaries but the epic-writer credits them with inordinately long life or persons belonging to these gotras are implied. Biographical sketches of these have been given in the footnotes of the previous volumes of this work.

devanagari: द्वितस्त्रितश्चैकतश्च ब्रह्मपुत्रास्तथाङ्गिराः । अगस्त्यो याज्ञवल्क्यश्च वामदेवादयोऽपरे ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: dvitastritaścaikataśca brahmaputrāstathāṅgirāḥagastyo yājñavalkyaśca vāmadevādayo'pare
translation: (They were as follows:) Kṛṣṇa-Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa), the divine sage Nārada, Cyavana, Devala, Asita, Viśvāmitra, Śatānanda, Bhāradvāja. Gautama, Paraśurāma along with his disciples, the worshipful Vasiṣṭha, Gālava, Bhṛgu, Pulastya, Kaśyapa, Atri, Mārkaṇḍeya, Bṛhaspati, Dvita, Trita, Ekata, sons of god Brahmā (such as Sanaka, Sanandana and others) Aṅgiras, Agastya, Yājñavalkya and others.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Under ‘others’ VT. includes Parāśara, Maitreya, Vaiśampāyana, etc. Historically these were not contemporaries but the epic-writer credits them with inordinately long life or persons belonging to these gotras are implied. Biographical sketches of these have been given in the footnotes of the previous volumes of this work.

devanagari: तान्दृष्ट्वा सहसोत्थाय प्रागासीना नृपादयः । पाण्डवाः कृष्णरामौ च प्रणेमुर्विश्ववन्दितान् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāndṛṣṭvā sahasotthāya prāgāsīnā nṛpādayaḥpāṇḍavāḥ kṛṣṇarāmau ca praṇemurviśvavanditān
translation: Beholding those sages who were universally respected, all the kings and others present there as well as Pāṇḍavas, Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma immediately rose from their seats to show respect and bowed to them.

devanagari: तान् आनर्चुर्यथा सर्वे सहरामोऽच्युतोऽर्चयत् । स्वागतासनपाद्यार्घ्य माल्यधूपानुलेपनैः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tān ānarcuryathā sarve saharāmo'cyuto'rcayatsvāgatāsanapādyārghya mālyadhūpānulepanaiḥ
translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa along with Balarāma and all others (present there) respectfully honoured the sages with sweet words of welcome, offering them seats, water to wash their hands and feet, flowers, incense, sandal paste, etc.

devanagari: उवाच सुखमासीनान् भगवान् धर्मगुप्तनुः । सदसस्तस्य महतो यतवाचोऽनुश्रृण्वतः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: uvāca sukhamāsīnān bhagavān dharmaguptanuḥsadasastasya mahato yatavāco'nuśrṛṇvataḥ
translation: When they were comfortably seated, the glorious Lord, whose (human) form (incarnated in this world) was for the protection of Dharma (Religious order or path of righteousness) addressed them, while the great assembly was silently listening to him.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । अहो वयं जन्मभृतो लब्धं कार्त्स्न्येन तत्फलम् । देवानामपि दुष्प्रापं यद् योगेश्वरदर्शनम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācaaho vayaṃ janmabhṛto labdhaṃ kārtsnyena tatphalamdevānāmapi duṣprāpaṃ yad yogeśvaradarśanam
translation:
The glorious Lord (Śrī Kṛṣṇa) said:
How blessed are we I Today we have attained completely the fruit (the highest objective) of having taken birth (in this world), as we have been blessed with a visit by masters of Yoga, the sight of whom is very difficult to obtain even for gods.

devanagari: किं स्वल्पतपसां नॄणां अर्चायां देवचक्षुषाम् । दर्शनस्पर्शनप्रश्न प्रह्वपादार्चनादिकम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ svalpatapasāṃ nṝṇāṃ arcāyāṃ devacakṣuṣāmdarśanasparśanapraśna prahvapādārcanādikam
translation: How can men whose (even concept of) penance is poor (limited to pilgrimage to sacred places, etc.) and who look upon the (stone or metal) images as God get the rare privilege of enjoying your sight, touching your feet), conversing with you, paying personal obeisance to you, worshipping your feet, etc.?

devanagari: न ह्यम्मयानि तीर्थानि न देवा मृच्छिलामयाः । ते पुनन्त्युरुकालेन दर्शनादेव साधवः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyammayāni tīrthāni na devā mṛcchilāmayāḥte punantyurukālena darśanādeva sādhavaḥ
translation: Waters in sacred places consisting of sheets of water are not the real holy waters; the images made of clay and stones are not the real gods. It is after a long course of time that they purify people. But saints like you purify instantly by their very sight.

devanagari: नाग्निर्न सूर्यो न च चन्द्रतारका । न भूर्जलं खं श्वसनोऽथ वाङ्मनः । उपासिता भेदकृतो हरन्त्यघं । विपश्चितो घ्नन्ति मुहूर्तसेवया ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: nāgnirna sūryo na ca candratārakāna bhūrjalaṃ khaṃ śvasano'tha vāṅmanaḥupāsitā bhedakṛto harantyaghaṃvipaścito ghnanti muhūrtasevayā
translation: If the deities presiding over the fire, sun, stars, planets, earth, water, heavens, air, speech and mind are worshipped and propitiated they cannot wipe out the sin (i.e. ignorance in spiritual matters which is the root cause of sins) but promote the notion of diversity and difference. But the service rendered to persons of spiritual wisdom, even for a short time (a muhūrta) eradicates it completely.

devanagari: यस्यात्मबुद्धिः कुणपे त्रिधातुके । स्वधीः कलत्रादिषु भौम इज्यधीः । यत्तीर्थबुद्धिः सलिले न कर्हिचित् । जनेष्वभिज्ञेषु स एव गोखरः ॥ १३ ...
english_devnagari: yasyātmabuddhiḥ kuṇape tridhātukesvadhīḥ kalatrādiṣu bhauma ijyadhīḥyattīrthabuddhiḥ salile na karhicitjaneṣvabhijñeṣu sa eva gokharaḥ
translation: He is really a donkey, carrying a fodder for the cattle[2] who looks upon this stinking dead body constituted of three humours (bile, phlegm and ‘air’) as the soul, regards wife and other relatives related to such a body as his own, believes a material image (of clay, stones, etc.) as an object of worship, and thinks that waters (absolve from sins and hence) are purificatory, but does not entertain such notions (of being saviours from sins) towards the spiritually wise.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
go-kharaḥ: VC. quotes Bṛhaspati Saṃhitā which states: Persons, even though paid respects by kings, should be regarded as ‘donkeys among brutes (gokharāḥ) if they do not know the religion (dharma) of the glorious Lord and the secrets of mantras.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । निशम्येत्थं भगवतः कृष्णस्याकुण्थमेधसः । वचो दुरन्वयं विप्राः तूष्णीमासन् भ्रमद्धियः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaniśamyetthaṃ bhagavataḥ kṛṣṇasyākuṇthamedhasaḥvaco duranvayaṃ viprāḥ tūṣṇīmāsan bhramaddhiyaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing this speech of the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa of unfailing infinite intelligence, which was very difficult to comprehend, the Brāhmaṇas got confounded and remained silent.

devanagari: चिरं विमृश्य मुनय ईश्वरस्येशितव्यताम् । जनसङ्ग्रह इत्यूचुः स्मयन्तस्तं जगद्गुरुम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ciraṃ vimṛśya munaya īśvarasyeśitavyatāmjanasaṅgraha ityūcuḥ smayantastaṃ jagadgurum
translation: Pondering over for a long time, they realized that though he is the Supreme Controller of the universe, it is for teaching the world by his own example, that he behaved like an ordinary person (under God’s control). And beaming with a smile, they addressed him.

devanagari: श्रीमुनय ऊचुः । यन्मायया तत्त्वविदुत्तमा वयं । विमोहिता विश्वसृजामधीश्वराः । यदीशितव्यायति गूढ ईहया । अहो विचित्रं भगवद् विचेष्टितम् ॥...
english_devnagari: śrīmunaya ūcuḥyanmāyayā tattvaviduttamā vayaṃvimohitā viśvasṛjāmadhīśvarāḥyadīśitavyāyati gūḍha īhayāaho vicitraṃ bhagavad viceṣṭitam
translation:
The sages said:
We, the best ones among the knowers of Truth and the chief among the progenitors of the world, are deluded by your Māyā, (Though you are the Lord of the Universe) you keep your universal rulership concealed by your apparent human acts, showing to be controlled by God’s authority. How wonderful is the behaviour of the Lord.

devanagari: अनीह एतद् बहुधैक आत्मना । सृजत्यवत्यत्ति न बध्यते यथा । भौमैर्हि भूमिर्बहुनामरूपिणी । अहो विभूम्नश्चरितं विडम्बनम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: anīha etad bahudhaika ātmanāsṛjatyavatyatti na badhyate yathābhaumairhi bhūmirbahunāmarūpiṇīaho vibhūmnaścaritaṃ viḍambanam
translation: Though he is one and no activity can be attributed to him (or whose activities are not determined or ordained by his Karmas), he appears in many names and forms just as the earth though one has many names and forms (e.g. a pot, a pitcher and others) through her own products[3] and (though one) by himself he creates, protects and eats up (i.e. withdraws within himself) the universe but is not bound by any of these acts. O! How mysterious is the working of (you) the Perfect One, whose (birth and other) acts are merely an (outward) imitation of human beings (The real you are different).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyaṃ mṛttiketyeva satyam /—Chāndogya. Up. 6.1.4.

devanagari: अथापि काले स्वजनाभिगुप्तये । बिभर्षि सत्त्वं खलनिग्रहाय च । स्वलीलया वेदपथं सनातनं । वर्णाश्रमात्मा पुरुषः परो भवान् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi kāle svajanābhiguptayebibharṣi sattvaṃ khalanigrahāya casvalīlayā vedapathaṃ sanātanaṃvarṇāśramātmā puruṣaḥ paro bhavān
translation: Even though Your Lordship is the Supreme Person (transcendental to Prakṛti and Puruṣa,) from time to time, you assume the pure sāttvika form for the protection of your devotees, and for the chastisement and control of the wicked, and by your example, you maintain the path of the Vedas—and you are the embodiment of different castes (varṇas) and stages in life (āśramas).

devanagari: ब्रह्म ते हृदयं शुक्लं तपःस्वाध्यायसंयमैः । यत्रोपलब्धं सद्व्यक्तं अव्यक्तं च ततः परम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: brahma te hṛdayaṃ śuklaṃ tapaḥsvādhyāyasaṃyamaiḥyatropalabdhaṃ sadvyaktaṃ avyaktaṃ ca tataḥ param
translation: The Veda constitutes your extremely pure heart. Through study, penance, self-control, you—the Brahman are realised there in both your manifest and unmanifest forms and your transcendental form is beyond the ken of these two.

devanagari: तस्माद् ब्रह्मकुलं ब्रह्मन् शास्त्रयोनेस्त्वमात्मनः । सभाजयसि सद्धाम तद् ब्रह्मण्याग्रणीर्भवान् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tasmād brahmakulaṃ brahman śāstrayonestvamātmanaḥsabhājayasi saddhāma tad brahmaṇyāgraṇīrbhavān
translation: O Lord Kṛṣṇa who are the Brahman yourself! As you are known through Śāstras (and Brāhmaṇas are the repository of these Śāstras) it is the Brāhmaṇas who form the best shrine as a means of realizing you. Hence, you are the most prominent amongst those who patronize the Brāhmaṇas and respect them

devanagari: अद्य नो जन्मसाफल्यं विद्यायास्तपसो दृशः । त्वया सङ्गम्य सद्गत्या यदन्तः श्रेयसां परः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: adya no janmasāphalyaṃ vidyāyāstapaso dṛśaḥtvayā saṅgamya sadgatyā yadantaḥ śreyasāṃ paraḥ
translation: Today in meeting you who are the shelter of all saintly persons, we have achieved the real benefit of our very existence, learning, penance and ability to see, as you are the highest consummation of all blessings.

devanagari: नमस्तस्मै भगवते कृष्णायाकुण्ठमेधसे । स्वयोगमाययाच्छन्न महिम्ने परमात्मने ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: namastasmai bhagavate kṛṣṇāyākuṇṭhamedhasesvayogamāyayācchanna mahimne paramātmane
translation: Salutation to you, O glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa of boundless wisdom and intelligence. You are the Supreme Soul who have shrouded your glory by your yogamāyā (yogic deluding potency).

devanagari: न यं विदन्त्यमी भूपा एकारामाश्च वृष्णयः । मायाजवनिकाच्छन्नं आत्मानं कालमीश्वरम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: na yaṃ vidantyamī bhūpā ekārāmāśca vṛṣṇayaḥmāyājavanikācchannaṃ ātmānaṃ kālamīśvaram
translation: These kings in the assembly and your clansmen (Vṛṣṇis) who share life and enjoyment together with you do not know that you are the Almighty God, the Time-spirit and the Supreme Soul concealed under the veil of your Māyā.

devanagari: यथा शयानः पुरुष आत्मानं गुणतत्त्वदृक् । नाममात्रेन्द्रियाभातं न वेद रहितं परम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā śayānaḥ puruṣa ātmānaṃ guṇatattvadṛknāmamātrendriyābhātaṃ na veda rahitaṃ param
translation: Just as a person who is asleep does not (during that stage) remember his body in his wakeful stage and looks upon all objects (he sees in the dream) as real and supposes his self in the dream which is perceptible to the mind and exists in name only, to be real, similarly, (even while awake), a person loses his power of discrimination as his sense-activities are directed towards objects which exist in name only (and are not real) by your Māyā and his mind being deluded and bewildered, he does not realize you.

devanagari: एवं त्वा नाममात्रेषु विषयेष्विन्द्रियेहया । मायया विभ्रमच्चित्तो न वेद स्मृत्युपप्लवात् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ tvā nāmamātreṣu viṣayeṣvindriyehayāmāyayā vibhramaccitto na veda smṛtyupaplavāt
translation: Just as a person who is asleep does not (during that stage) remember his body in his wakeful stage and looks upon all objects (he sees in the dream) as real and supposes his self in the dream which is perceptible to the mind and exists in name only, to be real, similarly, (even while awake), a person loses his power of discrimination as his sense-activities are directed towards objects which exist in name only (and are not real) by your Māyā and his mind being deluded and bewildered, he does not realize you.

devanagari: तस्याद्य ते ददृशिमाङ्घ्रिमघौघमर्ष । तीर्थास्पदं हृदि कृतं सुविपक्वयोगैः । उत्सिक्तभक्त्युपहताशय जीवकोशा । आपुर्भवद्गतिमथानुगृहान भक्ता...
english_devnagari: tasyādya te dadṛśimāṅghrimaghaughamarṣatīrthāspadaṃ hṛdi kṛtaṃ suvipakvayogaiḥutsiktabhaktyupahatāśaya jīvakośāāpurbhavadgatimathānugṛhāna bhaktān
translation: To-day we had the good fortune of seeking the feet of you (who are of the above description)—your feet which are the source of the sacred river Gaṅgā which absolves people of heaps of sins; which are enthroned in the heart by yogins whose yogic practices have been ripened and perfect. Those who have been able to wipe out evil proclivities or ego by means of overflowing tide of devotion have attained the highest stage of realizing you (or have secured resort in you). Hence, O Lord, be pleased to be gracious unto us your devotees.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्यनुज्ञाप्य दाशार्हं धृतराष्ट्रं युधिष्ठिरम् । राजर्षे स्वाश्रमान् गन्तुं मुनयो दधिरे मनः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaityanujñāpya dāśārhaṃ dhṛtarāṣṭraṃ yudhiṣṭhiramrājarṣe svāśramān gantuṃ munayo dadhire manaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus (prayed and) taken leave of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Dāśārha, Dhṛtarāṣṭra and Yudhiṣṭhira, the sages made up their minds to return to their respective hermitages, O royal sage.

devanagari: तद् वीक्ष्य तानुपव्रज्य वसुदेवो महायशाः । प्रणम्य चोपसङ्गृह्य बभाषेदं सुयन्त्रितः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tad vīkṣya tānupavrajya vasudevo mahāyaśāḥpraṇamya copasaṅgṛhya babhāṣedaṃ suyantritaḥ
translation: Seeing that the sages were departing, the far-famed Vasudeva approached them, bowed them, clasped their feet and with great self-restraint, he requested them as follows:

devanagari: श्रीवसुदेव उवाच । नमो वः सर्वदेवेभ्य ऋषयः श्रोतुमर्हथ । कर्मणा कर्मनिर्हारो यथा स्यान्नस्तदुच्यताम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvasudeva uvācanamo vaḥ sarvadevebhya ṛṣayaḥ śrotumarhathakarmaṇā karmanirhāro yathā syānnastaducyatām
translation:
Vasudeva said:
Salutation to you, O sages, in whom all deities abide.[4] It behoves you to listen to my request. Be pleased to advise me by adopting which course of karma, one can clear one’s self of all karmas.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes the following Vedic text in support of this supposition:
Yāvatīr vai devatās tāḥ sarvā veda-vidi brāhmaṇe vasanti /

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । नातिचित्रमिदं विप्रा वसुदेवो बुभुत्सया । कृष्णं मत्वार्भकं यन्नः पृच्छति श्रेय आत्मनः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācanāticitramidaṃ viprā vasudevo bubhutsayākṛṣṇaṃ matvārbhakaṃ yannaḥ pṛcchati śreya ātmanaḥ
translation:
Nārada said;
It is not strange or wonderful that Vasudeva, regard ing Śrī Kṛṣṇa as a child, is desirous of being enlightened in spiritual knowledge and is asking us about the way of attaining to his spiritual good.

devanagari: सन्निकर्षोऽत्र मर्त्यानां अनादरणकारणम् । गाङ्गं हित्वा यथान्याम्भः तत्रत्यो याति शुद्धये ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: sannikarṣo'tra martyānāṃ anādaraṇakāraṇamgāṅgaṃ hitvā yathānyāmbhaḥ tatratyo yāti śuddhaye
translation: In this world, over-familiarity in the case of mortals, breeds negligence or lack of reverence. Persons who live on the bank of the Gaṅgā discard the water of that very river and go to another place of sacred waters.

devanagari: यस्यानुभूतिः कालेन लयोत्पत्त्यादिनास्य वै । स्वतोऽन्यस्माच्च गुणतो न कुतश्चन रिष्यति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyānubhūtiḥ kālena layotpattyādināsya vaisvato'nyasmācca guṇato na kutaścana riṣyati
translation: Kṛṣṇa’s spiritual knowledge is not diminished by the force of Time which causes the creation and dissolution of the universe, nor by itself or by any external cause or action of guṇas.

devanagari: तं क्लेशकर्मपरिपाकगुणप्रवाहैः । अव्याहतानुभवमीश्वरमद्वितीयम् । प्राणादिभिः स्वविभवैरुपगूढमन्यो । मन्येत सूर्यमिव मेघहिमोपरागैः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ kleśakarmaparipākaguṇapravāhaiḥavyāhatānubhavamīśvaramadvitīyamprāṇādibhiḥ svavibhavairupagūḍhamanyomanyeta sūryamiva meghahimoparāgaiḥ
translation: His wisdom is unaffected by any affliction or hindrances (kleśa)[5] or the effect of karmas or flow of guṇas. He is the Almighty Lord, one without a second. But ordinary people regard him just like themselves[6] as he has shrouded himself under his own potencies, vital airs, etc., just as the sun is concealed by clouds, mists and eclipse, etc.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Patañjali enumerates the following kleśas:
Nescience (avidyā), feeling-of-personality or ego (asmita), attachment (rāga), aversion (dveṣa) and will-to-live or fear of death (abhiniveśa).

Footnote [6]:
Padaratnāvalī Vasudeva being an ordinary person regards the Almighty Lord Kṛṣṇa just a common person like himself, as Kṛṣṇa has shrouded himself under his own potencies, etc.

devanagari: अथोचुर्मुनयो राजन् आभाष्यानकदुंदुभिम् । सर्वेषां श्रृणतां राज्ञां तथैवाच्युतरामयोः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: athocurmunayo rājan ābhāṣyānakaduṃdubhimsarveṣāṃ śrṛṇatāṃ rājñāṃ tathaivācyutarāmayoḥ
translation: Then, O king, the sages, addressed Vasudeva (Ānakadundubhi) as follows while all including Acyuta and Balarāma, were hearing them.

devanagari: कर्मणा कर्मनिर्हार एष साधुनिरूपितः । यच्छ्रद्धया यजेद् विष्णुं सर्वयज्ञेश्वरं मखैः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: karmaṇā karmanirhāra eṣa sādhunirūpitaḥyacchraddhayā yajed viṣṇuṃ sarvayajñeśvaraṃ makhaiḥ
translation: “The cause of wiping out of karmas through performance of karmas has been seen (and declared) well by the good. One should devoutly propitiate and worship Viṣṇu, the Lord of sacrifices by performing sacrifice.

devanagari: चित्तस्योपशमोऽयं वै कविभिः शास्त्रचक्षुषा । दर्शितः सुगमो योगो धर्मश्चात्ममुदावहः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: cittasyopaśamo'yaṃ vai kavibhiḥ śāstracakṣuṣādarśitaḥ sugamo yogo dharmaścātmamudāvahaḥ
translation: This course has been shown as the way to serenity of mind, an easy type of Yoga and dharma that brings delight to the soul, by wise sages who perceive with the eye of śāstras.

devanagari: अयं स्वस्त्ययनः पन्था द्विजातेर्गृहमेधिनः । यच्छ्रद्धयाऽप्तवित्तेन शुक्लेनेज्येत पूरुषः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ svastyayanaḥ panthā dvijātergṛhamedhinaḥyacchraddhayā'ptavittena śuklenejyeta pūruṣaḥ
translation: It has been regarded as the most auspicious course for householders of the twice-born castes, that they should devoutly worship the Supreme Person by sacrifices performed with pure self-earned wealth with fair means.

devanagari: वित्तैषणां यज्ञदानैः गृहैर्दारसुतैषणाम् । आत्मलोकैषणां देव कालेन विसृजेद्बुधः । ग्रामे त्यक्तैषणाः सर्वे ययुर्धीरास्तपोवनम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: vittaiṣaṇāṃ yajñadānaiḥ gṛhairdārasutaiṣaṇāmātmalokaiṣaṇāṃ deva kālena visṛjedbudhaḥgrāme tyaktaiṣaṇāḥ sarve yayurdhīrāstapovanam
translation: One should give up covetousness for wealth by spending it in the performance of sacrifices, and in gifts, the desire for wife and children by enjoying conjugal happiness in married life, the aspiration for celestial world and the pleasures thereof by considering that they are subject to damaging effects of Time, leaving all these (three) desires at home in one’s village, the wise have proceeded to the forest for performance of penance, O Vasudeva.

devanagari: ऋणैस्त्रिभिर्द्विजो जातो देवर्षिपितॄणां प्रभो । यज्ञाध्ययनपुत्रैस्तानि अनिस्तीर्य त्यजन्पतेत् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṇaistribhirdvijo jāto devarṣipitṝṇāṃ prabhoyajñādhyayanaputraistāni anistīrya tyajanpatet
translation: O Lord Vasudeva, a twice-born person is born with three debts which one owes to gods, sages and manes. If one were to renounce the household life without repaying these debts by performance of sacrifice, Vedic studies and begetting sons, he is sure to fall.

devanagari: त्वं त्वद्य मुक्तो द्वाभ्यां वै ऋषिपित्रोर्महामते । यज्ञैर्देवर्णमुन्मुच्य निरृणोऽशरणो भव ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṃ tvadya mukto dvābhyāṃ vai ṛṣipitrormahāmateyajñairdevarṇamunmucya nirṛṇo'śaraṇo bhava
translation: You are now free from two debts, O highly intelligent Vasudeva, the obligations to sages and to the manes (pitṛs). Now discharging the debt to gods through performance of sacrifices, absolve yourself of all obligations and be a house-less recluse.

devanagari: वसुदेव भवान्नूनं भक्त्या परमया हरिम् । जगतामीश्वरं प्रार्चः स यद्वां पुत्रतां गतः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: vasudeva bhavānnūnaṃ bhaktyā paramayā harimjagatāmīśvaraṃ prārcaḥ sa yadvāṃ putratāṃ gataḥ
translation: O Vasudeva, you have with the most intense devotion worshipped Hari, the Lord of the universe. He has become your son.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा वसुदेवो महामनाः । तान् ऋषीन् ऋत्विजो वव्रे मूर्ध्नाऽनम्य प्रसाद्य च ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaiti tadvacanaṃ śrutvā vasudevo mahāmanāḥtān ṛṣīn ṛtvijo vavre mūrdhnā'namya prasādya ca
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing the words of the sages, the noble-minded Vasudeva bowed those sages with his head and beseeched them for their favour and requested them to officiate as priests for his sacrifice.

devanagari: त एनमृषयो राजन् वृता धर्मेण धार्मिकम् । तस्मिन् अयाजयन् क्षेत्रे मखैरुत्तमकल्पकैः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: ta enamṛṣayo rājan vṛtā dharmeṇa dhārmikamtasmin ayājayan kṣetre makhairuttamakalpakaiḥ
translation: Those sages who were appointed with proper religious formalities, assisted the righteous-minded Vasudeva to perform a number of sacrifices in the best possible manner (or on the largest scale) in that holy place, O King Parīkṣit.

devanagari: तद्दीक्षायां प्रवृत्तायां वृष्णयः पुष्करस्रजः । स्नाताः सुवाससो राजन् राजानः सुष्ठ्वलङ्कृताः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: taddīkṣāyāṃ pravṛttāyāṃ vṛṣṇayaḥ puṣkarasrajaḥsnātāḥ suvāsaso rājan rājānaḥ suṣṭhvalaṅkṛtāḥ
translation: When Vasudeva was being initiated for the sacrifice, O King, members of the Vṛṣṇi clan and other kings took their bath, dressed themselves in excellent clothes, wore wreaths of lotuses and adorned themselves with ornaments. Vasudeva’s queens also who had besmeared their persons with (fragrant) pigments, put on gold necklaces and clad in their best apparel, came to the sacrificial hall with offerings in their hands.

devanagari: तन्महिष्यश्च मुदिता निष्ककण्ठ्यः सुवाससः । दीक्षाशालामुपाजग्मुः आलिप्ता वस्तुपाणयः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tanmahiṣyaśca muditā niṣkakaṇṭhyaḥ suvāsasaḥdīkṣāśālāmupājagmuḥ āliptā vastupāṇayaḥ
translation: When Vasudeva was being initiated for the sacrifice, O King, members of the Vṛṣṇi clan and other kings took their bath, dressed themselves in excellent clothes, wore wreaths of lotuses and adorned themselves with ornaments. Vasudeva’s queens also who had besmeared their persons with (fragrant) pigments, put on gold necklaces and clad in their best apparel, came to the sacrificial hall with offerings in their hands.

devanagari: नेदुर्मृदङ्गपटह शङ्खभेर्यानकादयः । ननृतुर्नटनर्तक्यः तुष्टुवुः सूतमागधाः । जगुः सुकण्ठ्यो गन्धर्व्यः संगीतं सहभर्तृकाः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: nedurmṛdaṅgapaṭaha śaṅkhabheryānakādayaḥnanṛturnaṭanartakyaḥ tuṣṭuvuḥ sūtamāgadhāḥjaguḥ sukaṇṭhyo gandharvyaḥ saṃgītaṃ sahabhartṛkāḥ
translation: Musical instruments like Mṛdaṅgas, tabors, conchs, drums and kettle drums were sounded. Actors (male) and female dancers danced. Sūtas and Māgadhas sang the panegyrics. Sweet-voiced Gandharva ladies along with husbands presented vocal music.

devanagari: तमभ्यषिञ्चन् विधिवद् अक्तं अभ्यक्तमृत्विजः । पत्‍नीभिरष्टादशभिः सोमराजमिवोडुभिः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: tamabhyaṣiñcan vidhivad aktaṃ abhyaktamṛtvijaḥpat‍nībhiraṣṭādaśabhiḥ somarājamivoḍubhiḥ
translation: Collyrium was applied to Vasudeva’s eyes. His body was besmeared with butter. The sacrificial priests with due formalities consecrated with sprinkling water Vasudeva along with his eighteen queens, as the moon god was formerly consecrated in the company of his (twentyseven) consorts, in ancient times.

devanagari: ताभिर्दुकूलवलयैः हारनूपुरकुण्डलैः । स्वलङ्कृताभिर्विबभौ दीक्षितोऽजिनसंवृतः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: tābhirdukūlavalayaiḥ hāranūpurakuṇḍalaiḥsvalaṅkṛtābhirvibabhau dīkṣito'jinasaṃvṛtaḥ
translation: The consecrated Vasudeva who was clad in deerskin, appeared extremely splendid with his queens, all of whom were clad in silken garments and were excellently adorned with gold bangles, pearl necklaces, anklets and ear-rings.

devanagari: तस्यर्त्विजो महाराज रत्‍नकौशेयवाससः । ससदस्या विरेजुस्ते यथा वृत्रहणोऽध्वरे ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyartvijo mahārāja rat‍nakauśeyavāsasaḥsasadasyā virejuste yathā vṛtrahaṇo'dhvare
translation: Wearing silk garments and jewels, the priest, who officiated at Vasudeva’s sacrifice along with the members of the sacrificial assembly, appeared splendid like the corresponding priests in Indra’s sacrifice, O great king.

devanagari: तदा रामश्च कृष्णश्च स्वैः स्वैः बन्धुभिरन्वितौ । रेजतुः स्वसुतैर्दारैः जीवेशौ स्वविभूतिभिः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: tadā rāmaśca kṛṣṇaśca svaiḥ svaiḥ bandhubhiranvitaurejatuḥ svasutairdāraiḥ jīveśau svavibhūtibhiḥ
translation: At that time, Balarāma and Śrī Kṛṣṇa (the former representing the jīva and the latter the Almighty Lord) the rulers of living beings, accompanied with their brothers, sons and consorts shone forth with all their supreme glorious powers (vibhūtis).

devanagari: ईजेऽनुयज्ञं विधिना अग्निहोत्रादिलक्षणैः । प्राकृतैर्वैकृतैर्यज्ञैः द्रव्यज्ञानक्रियेश्वरम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: īje'nuyajñaṃ vidhinā agnihotrādilakṣaṇaiḥprākṛtairvaikṛtairyajñaiḥ dravyajñānakriyeśvaram
translation: In every sacrifice, by performing with due rites and formalities, the Prākṛta[7] and Vaikṛta categories of sacrifices characterised by Agnihotra, he worshipped Lord Viṣṇu who is the controller and master of the materials for the sacrifice, the knowledge of Mantras employed and the. formal act of performing the sacrifice.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: Prākṛta Jyotiṣṭoma, Darśa, Pūrṇamāsa, Vaikṛtta: saura sattra and others going under the name Agnihotra.

devanagari: अथर्त्विग्भ्योऽददात्काले यथाम्नातं स दक्षिणाः । स्वलङ्कृतेभ्योऽलङ्कृत्य गोभूकन्या महाधनाः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: athartvigbhyo'dadātkāle yathāmnātaṃ sa dakṣiṇāḥsvalaṅkṛtebhyo'laṅkṛtya gobhūkanyā mahādhanāḥ
translation: Then at the appropriate time as prescribed in the Śāstras, Vasudeva gave to his sacrificial priests who were already adorned with ornaments, very richly[8] adorned cows, girls and land as the sacrificial fee.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
The v.l. mahādhanaḥ means ‘the extremely affluent Vasudeva’. VT. takes ‘Mahādhanāḥ’ as qualifying Dakṣiṇāḥ and interprets, ‘sacrificial fees consisting of costly jewels, gold, money, etc.’

devanagari: पत्‍नीसंयाजावभृथ्यैः चरित्वा ते महर्षयः । सस्नू रामह्रदे विप्रा यजमानपुरःसराः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: pat‍nīsaṃyājāvabhṛthyaiḥ caritvā te maharṣayaḥsasnū rāmahrade viprā yajamānapuraḥsarāḥ
translation: Having made the sacrificer’s wife to perform the Samyāja part of the sacrifice and after the rites of avabhṛtha ablution, the great sages who officiated at the sacrifice followed the Yajamāna (the performer of sacrifice) to the pools (created by and known after the name) of Paraśurāma and performed the concluding bath.

devanagari: स्नातोऽलङ्कारवासांसि वन्दिभ्योऽदात्तथा स्त्रियः । ततः स्वलङ्कृतो वर्णान् आश्वभ्योऽन्नेन पूजयत् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: snāto'laṅkāravāsāṃsi vandibhyo'dāttathā striyaḥtataḥ svalaṅkṛto varṇān āśvabhyo'nnena pūjayat
translation: After taking his bath, Vasudeva richly adorned himself with ornaments and distributed clothes and ornaments to bards and his queens also gave gifts. He then entertained with food, people of all castes and communities and animals down to dogs to their complete satisfaction.

devanagari: बन्धून् सदारान् ससुतान् पारिबर्हेण भूयसा । विदर्भकोशलकुरून् काशिकेकय सृञ्जयान् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: bandhūn sadārān sasutān pāribarheṇa bhūyasāvidarbhakośalakurūn kāśikekaya sṛñjayān
translation: With rich liberal presents, Vasudeva honoured his relatives, their wives, and children—the princes and people of Vidarbha, Kosala, Kuru, Kāśi, Kekaya and Sṛñjayas; and also the members of the sacrificial assembly and the priests; various categories of gods, men, bhūtaṣ, pitṛs (manes) and Cāraṇas. All of them took leave of Kṛṣṇa, the abode of the goddess Śrī and praising him and the sacrifice, returned to (their respective) home.

devanagari: सदस्यर्त्विक्सुरगणान् नृभूतपितृचारणान् । श्रीनिकेतमनुज्ञाप्य शंसन्तः प्रययुः क्रतुम् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: sadasyartviksuragaṇān nṛbhūtapitṛcāraṇānśrīniketamanujñāpya śaṃsantaḥ prayayuḥ kratum
translation: With rich liberal presents, Vasudeva honoured his relatives, their wives, and children—the princes and people of Vidarbha, Kosala, Kuru, Kāśi, Kekaya and Sṛñjayas; and also the members of the sacrificial assembly and the priests; various categories of gods, men, bhūtaṣ, pitṛs (manes) and Cāraṇas. All of them took leave of Kṛṣṇa, the abode of the goddess Śrī and praising him and the sacrifice, returned to (their respective) home.

devanagari: धृतराष्ट्रोऽनुजः पार्था भीष्मो द्रोणः पृथा यमौ । नारदो भगवान् व्यासः सुहृत्संबंधिबांधवाः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: dhṛtarāṣṭro'nujaḥ pārthā bhīṣmo droṇaḥ pṛthā yamaunārado bhagavān vyāsaḥ suhṛtsaṃbaṃdhibāṃdhavāḥ
translation: Dhṛtarāṣṭra and his younger brother Vidura, the sons of Pṛthā, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kuntī and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), Nārada, the glorious sage Vyāsa, friends, relatives and kinsmen with their hearts softened with friendly feelings, embraced their relatives of the Yadu clan and with heart heavy with the pangs of separation, left for their own states along with other people.

devanagari: बन्धून्परिष्वज्य यदून् सौहृदाक्लिन्नचेतसः । ययुर्विरहकृच्छ्रेण स्वदेशांश्चापरे जनाः ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: bandhūnpariṣvajya yadūn sauhṛdāklinnacetasaḥyayurvirahakṛcchreṇa svadeśāṃścāpare janāḥ
translation: Dhṛtarāṣṭra and his younger brother Vidura, the sons of Pṛthā, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kuntī and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), Nārada, the glorious sage Vyāsa, friends, relatives and kinsmen with their hearts softened with friendly feelings, embraced their relatives of the Yadu clan and with heart heavy with the pangs of separation, left for their own states along with other people.

devanagari: नन्दस्तु सह गोपालैः बृहत्या पूजयार्चितः । कृष्णरामोग्रसेनाद्यैः न्यवात्सीद्बन्धुवत्सलः ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: nandastu saha gopālaiḥ bṛhatyā pūjayārcitaḥkṛṣṇarāmograsenādyaiḥ nyavātsīdbandhuvatsalaḥ
translation: Highly honoured with extensive rich presents by Kṛṣṇa, Balarāma, Ugrasena and others, Nanda who was very affectionate to them, stayed on along with the Gopas (for some time more).

devanagari: वसुदेवोऽञ्जसोत्तीर्य मनोरथमहार्णवम् । सुहृद्वृतः प्रीतमना नन्दमाह करे स्पृशन् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: vasudevo'ñjasottīrya manorathamahārṇavamsuhṛdvṛtaḥ prītamanā nandamāha kare spṛśan
translation: Having thus crossed the ocean of his desire (of performing sacrifices) with such an ease, Vasudeva was greatly delighted at heart. Surrounded by his friends and well-wishers, he took Nanda by the hand and said:

devanagari: श्रीवसुदेव उवाच । भ्रातरीशकृतः पाशो नृनां यः स्नेहसंज्ञितः । तं दुस्त्यजमहं मन्ये शूराणामपि योगिनाम् ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvasudeva uvācabhrātarīśakṛtaḥ pāśo nṛnāṃ yaḥ snehasaṃjñitaḥtaṃ dustyajamahaṃ manye śūrāṇāmapi yoginām
translation:
Vasudeva said:
O brother Nanda! A bond called friendly affection has been forged by the Almighty Lord. I think it is very difficult even for valiant warriors and yogins to snap it asunder,

devanagari: अस्मास्वप्रतिकल्पेयं यत्कृताज्ञेषु सत्तमैः । मैत्र्यर्पिताफला चापि न निवर्तेत कर्हिचित् ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: asmāsvapratikalpeyaṃ yatkṛtājñeṣu sattamaiḥmaitryarpitāphalā cāpi na nivarteta karhicit
translation: For, the great friendliness extended by the virtuous- most persons like you to ungrateful persons like us is unsurpassed and too valuable to be repaid. Though your friendliness bears no reciprocal return, it will never be withdrawn from us.

devanagari: प्रागकल्पाच्च कुशलं भ्रातर्वो नाचराम हि । अधुना श्रीमदान्धाक्षा न पश्यामः पुरः सतः ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: prāgakalpācca kuśalaṃ bhrātarvo nācarāma hiadhunā śrīmadāndhākṣā na paśyāmaḥ puraḥ sataḥ
translation: We, being ourself helpless and simply incapable of doing it, did not do any good turn to you. And now our eyes being blinded by the intoxication of affluence, we do not see you even when you are before us, O brother.

devanagari: मा राज्यश्रीरभूत्पुंसः श्रेयस्कामस्य मानद । स्वजनानुत बन्धून् वा न पश्यति ययान्धदृक् ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: mā rājyaśrīrabhūtpuṃsaḥ śreyaskāmasya mānadasvajanānuta bandhūn vā na paśyati yayāndhadṛk
translation: O respectable brother, royal fortune should not come to the lot of a. person who aspires after the final beatitude, for his eyesight gets blinded by it and he dots not recognize his people or relatives.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं सौहृदशैथिल्य चित्त आनकदुन्दुभिः । रुरोद तत्कृतां मैत्रीं स्मरन् अश्रुविलोचनः ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaevaṃ sauhṛdaśaithilya citta ānakadundubhiḥruroda tatkṛtāṃ maitrīṃ smaran aśruvilocanaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, the heart of Vasudeva was softened with the feelings of friendliness. Remembering the warm friendship borne by Nanda to him, tears welled-up in his eyes and he wept (with tears gushing down his cheeks).

devanagari: नन्दस्तु सख्युः प्रियकृत् प्रेम्णा गोविन्दरामयोः । अद्य श्व इति मासांस्त्रीन् यदुभिर्मानितोऽवसत् ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: nandastu sakhyuḥ priyakṛt premṇā govindarāmayoḥadya śva iti māsāṃstrīn yadubhirmānito'vasat
translation: With a view to giving the pleasure of his company to Vasudeva and out of deep affection from Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, he postponed his departure from day to day and stayed on there for three months more, respectfully treated all the while by the Yadus.

devanagari: ततः कामैः पूर्यमाणः सव्रजः सहबान्धवः । परार्ध्याभरणक्षौम नानानर्घ्यपरिच्छदैः ॥ ६७ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kāmaiḥ pūryamāṇaḥ savrajaḥ sahabāndhavaḥparārdhyābharaṇakṣauma nānānarghyaparicchadaiḥ
translation: During his stay, Nanda together with relatives (Gopas) and the inhabitants of Vraja (who accompanied him there) were catered to their heart’s content, whatever they desired and wanted, by offering them valuable ornaments, silk cloths and other costly presents.

devanagari: वसुदेवोग्रसेनाभ्यां कृष्णोद्धवबलादिभिः । दत्तमादाय पारिबर्हं यापितो यदुभिर्ययौ ॥ ६८ ॥
english_devnagari: vasudevograsenābhyāṃ kṛṣṇoddhavabalādibhiḥdattamādāya pāribarhaṃ yāpito yadubhiryayau
translation: Taking with him all the valuable gifts presented to him by Vasudeva, Ugrasena, Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Uddhava, Balarāma and others, he took leave of the Yādavas and returned home (with a big Yādava army to guard him during his return journey).

devanagari: नन्दो गोपाश्च गोप्यश्च गोविन्दचरणाम्बुजे । मनः क्षिप्तं पुनर्हर्तुं अनीशा मथुरां ययुः ॥ ६९ ॥
english_devnagari: nando gopāśca gopyaśca govindacaraṇāmbujemanaḥ kṣiptaṃ punarhartuṃ anīśā mathurāṃ yayuḥ
translation: And the noble-souled Pāṇḍavas and Kuntī, speaking in faltering accents (as their voice was choked with tears) accompanied with all their relatives, returned to Indraprastha, taking leave of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: बन्धुषु प्रतियातेषु वृष्णयः कृष्णदेवताः । वीक्ष्य प्रावृषमासन्नाद् ययुर्द्वारवतीं पुनः ॥ ७० ॥
english_devnagari: bandhuṣu pratiyāteṣu vṛṣṇayaḥ kṛṣṇadevatāḥvīkṣya prāvṛṣamāsannād yayurdvāravatīṃ punaḥ
translation: When all the relatives departed (to their respective places), Vṛṣṇis who looked upon Kṛṣṇa as their Deity, noticed the approach of the monsoons and returned to Dvārakā.

devanagari: जनेभ्यः कथयां चक्रुः यदुदेवमहोत्सवम् । यदासीत् तीर्थयात्रायां सुहृत् संदर्शनादिकम् ॥ ७१ ॥
english_devnagari: janebhyaḥ kathayāṃ cakruḥ yadudevamahotsavamyadāsīt tīrthayātrāyāṃ suhṛt saṃdarśanādikam
translation:
On their return, they narrated to the people (who could not come to that pilgrimage) the great festival of the sacrifice performed by Vasudeva, the leader of Yadus and their meeting of friends and other incidents during the course of that pilgrimage.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । तीर्थयात्रानुवर्णनं नाम चतुरशीतितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ८४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhetīrthayātrānuvarṇanaṃ nāma caturaśītitamo'dhyāyaḥ
